posts.title: SNARK DESIGN AND THEIR ROLE IN ROLLUPS(HOW YOU CAN BUILD A SETUP TO SCALE TX.ON ETHEREUM & BUILD PRIVACY PRESERVING APPLICATIONS)
posts.excerpt: A snark is a type of zero-knowledge proof, which is a cryptographic technique that allows one to prove that a statement is true without revealing any information beyond the validity of the statement. For example, one can prove that they know a password without revealing the password itself.
posts.content:
The front end of snarks is the part of the system that allows users to write programs or statements that can be compiled into snark circuits. A snark circuit is a representation of a computation that can be verified using a snark proof, which is a short and non-interactive proof that the computation was done correctly without revealing any details about it.

The front end of snarks interacts with high level languages by providing tools and features that enable users to express their programs or statements in a more natural and intuitive way than using low level languages or libraries.

Click to read more

posts.title: ZERO KNOWLEDGE PROOFS AND 🧬 EVOLUTION IN THEIR MODELS.
posts.excerpt: As Weu foundation team and Dev's are working and researching around zk proofs and 🆔 infrastructure. As we are building a zk proof based GitHub , where Dev's can contribute being anonymous and get direct incentives to their dashboard.
posts.content:
The same way zk proof has two main properties which are soundness and zero knowledge as the name suggests. Today will go through the whole evolution 🧬 cycle of zk proofs.

Zero-knowledge proofs are a fascinating topic in cryptography. They are a way of proving that a certain statement is true without revealing any additional information beyond the truth of the statement itself. For example, you can prove that you know the password to a website without actually typing it or showing it to anyone. Zero-knowledge proofs have many applications in security, privacy, and authentication.

posts.title: Proposer Builder separation at Protocol Level
posts.excerpt: A blockchain is a data structure that stores a sequence of transactions in a secure and decentralized way.
posts.content:
A blockchain is a data structure that stores a sequence of transactions in a secure and decentralized way. Each transaction is grouped into a block, which contains a unique identifier (hash), a reference to the previous block (previous hash), and other metadata. The blocks are linked together by their hashes, forming a chain of blocks. This chain is verified and maintained by a network of nodes that follow a consensus protocol. The blockchain data structure ensures that the transactions are immutable, transparent, and verifiable.

Click to read more

posts.title: SMR | Tendermint ( Partial Synchronous)| PERMISSIONLESS SETTINGS AROUND A CONSENSUS PROTOCOL
posts.excerpt: This blog will take you through these questions are SMR, TENDERMINT AND PERMISSIONLESS SETTINGS.
posts.content:
A technique where each node maintains a copy of a state machine, and they need to execute the same sequence of commands on their state machines in order to keep them consistent. For example, a bank system may use SMR to ensure that all the branches have the same balance for each account.

Let's take a poker game example with Alice, Bob, Carol and Dave.

One possible creative example around state machine replication technique in blockchain is a story about a group of friends who want to play a game of poker online. Here is how it goes:

Alice, Bob, Carol, and Dave are four friends who love to play poker. They decide to use a blockchain-based platform to play a game of poker online, without trusting any third-party service. They each download a poker app that implements state machine replication (SMR) to ensure that the game is fair and consistent, even if some of them are dishonest or offline.

Click to read more

posts.title: CONSENSUS DESIGN AROUND BYZANTINE PROBLEM
posts.excerpt: A fundamental problem in distributed computing and multi-agent systems is to achieve overall system reliability in the presence of a number of faulty processes.
posts.content:
A fundamental problem in distributed computing and multi-agent systems is to achieve overall system reliability in the presence of a number of faulty processes. This often requires coordinating processes to reach consensus, or agree on some data value that is needed during computation. Example applications of consensus include agreeing on what transactions to commit to a database in which order, state machine replication, and atomic broadcasts. Real-world applications often requiring consensus include cloud computing, clock synchronization, PageRank, opinion formation, smart power grids, state estimation, control of UAVs (and multiple robots/agents in general), load balancing, blockchain, and others.

Click to read more

posts.title: MEV, FLASHBOTS & SUAVE
posts.excerpt: Maximal extractable value (MEV) is the maximum profit that a miner or validator can make by including, excluding, or changing the order of transactions in a block on a blockchain network.
posts.content:
Maximal extractable value (MEV) is the maximum profit that a miner or validator can make by including, excluding, or changing the order of transactions in a block on a blockchain network. MEV can affect the security, fairness, and efficiency of the network, as well as the user experience and fees. MEV can be extracted by various methods, such as front-running, back-running, sandwich attacks, and block stuffing. MEV is a phenomenon that exists on any blockchain that supports smart contracts, such as Ethereum. Here we are going to discuss about these aspects in this blog where did mev come from , aspects around flashbots and mev opportunities and how the future looks like with SUAVE……

Click to read more

posts.title: It's All About Trust
posts.excerpt: Cryptography, distributed computing and economics are three key aspects of blockchain technology that play a role in improving the existing trust system around blockchains. Here is a brief overview of how they do so:
posts.content:
Cryptography: Cryptography is the science of securing information using mathematical techniques. Cryptography enables blockchains to protect the data stored in the ledger from unauthorized access, modification or deletion. Cryptography also allows blockchains to verify the identity and authenticity of the participants and transactions in the network. For example, blockchains use cryptographic hash functions to generate unique identifiers for each block and transaction, and digital signatures to ensure that only the rightful owners can spend their coins or tokens.

Distributed computing: Distributed computing enables blockchains to operate in a decentralized manner, without relying on a central authority or intermediary. Distributed computing also allows blockchains to achieve consensus among the nodes in the network, which means that they can agree on the state and validity of the ledger. For example, blockchains use various consensus algorithms, such as proof-of-work, proof-of-stake, proof-of-authority, etc., to coordinate the nodes and prevent malicious attacks or forks.

Click to read more

posts.title: Proof of stake consensus survey
posts.excerpt: Proof of stake (PoS) is a type of consensus mechanism that uses the stake of the participants, or validators, to secure the network and produce new blocks in the blockchain. Validators need to deposit a certain amount of coins, or tokens
posts.content:
Proof of stake consensus survey

Proof of work

Proof of work (PoW) is a type of consensus mechanism that uses the computational power of the participants, or miners, to secure the network and produce new blocks in the blockchain. Miners need to solve a hard mathematical puzzle, or proof-of-work, to create a valid block and receive a reward in crypto. The more computational power a miner has, the higher the chance they have to solve the puzzle and create the next block. Miners compete with each other to find the solution, which ensures that the network is decentralized and resistant to attacks.

Click to read more

INTRODUCTION

Koboto is a Self-coordinated Agentic network for Heterogenous systems. An agentic network with dynamic and modular compute, verifiability and access of AI agents while providing heterogenous environment for users. Koboto.ai is a modular & heterogenous AI network compose with peer to peer abstract intelligent incentive driven agent market to produce multi-interactive, inference aggregator and intent based actions while stacking adaptive compute, verifiability and access of heterogenous states. Heterogeneity brings flexibility and fast adaptability of agencies participating in the environment. Thus, koboto network solve it by introducing heterogenous systems equipped with heterogenous reputation, heterogenous states, heterogenous resources, heterogenous compute and heterogenous calls. Intelligence production has many substrate like how it is being produced , with what resources ; with what topology ; with what extention of diversified producers ; with what technique to optimize or aggregate it and the list goes on from general training to fine-tuning to get feedbacks from inferential models to fit back in to produced one more better & bigger models satisfiying certain benchmarks in coding , language , maths , text generation , vision & so on .

In the current paradigm of open sourcing the model weights where the bottelnecks for companies to general ML peeps is the cost to train the models is in millions of $ , most of the ml community is been shifting to the post training era of models and generally towards fine tuning models with which field they are interested in , prompt strategies , agentic frameworks , data curation and so on . Meanwhile companies and open source projects seemlessly focusing on model hosting platform (FT models as well) , datasets providers , ML tooling , decentralised / heterogenous compute infrastructure providers , federated learning (where individuals share only models weights not the raw data ) , a few communities & projects focusing on merging Crypto x AI where it's a merge of decentralised intelligence backed by economic trust foundation where projects like coordination networks , intelligence producing networks , Agents & Agentic frameworks , Data DAOs across various tasks alignment from regression to classification to generative AI coming to fruitation solving tasks around Defi , Asset management , Market movements , prediction , MEV , crypto specific FT models for text generation , NFTs to even extending arms towards solving things for science like protein folding , disease pre-identification , personalised medicines , skin disease classification ; Agentic ontologies for companies , threat detection in code and physical infrastructure , embodied AI and so on…….
